  " map Code property in extended entity to Code property in created value help entity
  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'LocalDataProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_property_path( 'inventory_loc' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'inv_loc' ).


**********************************************************************
* Plant Value Help                                                   *
**********************************************************************

  DATA(plant_property) = model->get_entity_type( iv_entity_name = 'C_InquiryWL_F2370Type' )->get_property( iv_property_name =  'Werks' ).

  plant_property->set_value_list( iv_value_list_type = /iwbep/if_mgw_odata_property=>gcs_value_list_type_property-standard ).

  vh_target = vocab_anno_model->create_annotations_target( iv_target = 'SD_F2370_INQY_WL_SRV.C_InquiryWL_F2370Type/Werks' ).
  vh_annotation = vh_target->create_annotation( 'Common.ValueList' ).
  vh_annotation->set_value_list_et_name( 'I_PlantVHType' ). "Entity Type
  vh_anno_record = vh_annotation->create_record( ).


  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'CollectionPath' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'I_PlantVH' ). "Entity Set
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'SearchSupported' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_boolean( abap_true ).


  " parameter list
  vh_collection = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'Parameters' )->create_collection( ) ##NO_TEXT.

  " map Code property in extended entity to Code property in created value help entity
  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'LocalDataProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_property_path( 'Werks' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'Plant' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'PlantName' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'SalesOrganization' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'DistributionChannel' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'Division' ).


  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'PlantSupplier' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'PlantCustomer' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'PlantCategory' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'FactoryCalendar' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'ValuationArea' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'DefaultPurchasingOrganization' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'AddressID' ).




**********************************************************************
* BaseCode Value Help                                                *
**********************************************************************


  DATA(basecode_property) = model->get_entity_type( iv_entity_name = 'C_InquiryWL_F2370Type' )->get_property( iv_property_name =  'Matnr' ).

  basecode_property->set_value_list( iv_value_list_type = /iwbep/if_mgw_odata_property=>gcs_value_list_type_property-standard ).

  vh_target = vocab_anno_model->create_annotations_target( iv_target = 'SD_F2370_INQY_WL_SRV.C_InquiryWL_F2370Type/Matnr' ).
  vh_annotation = vh_target->create_annotation( 'Common.ValueList' ).
  vh_annotation->set_value_list_et_name( 'C_MassUpdtMaterialVHType' ). "Entity Type
  vh_anno_record = vh_annotation->create_record( ).


  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'CollectionPath' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'C_MassUpdtMaterialVH' ). "Entity Set
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'SearchSupported' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_boolean( abap_true ).


  " parameter list
  vh_collection = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'Parameters' )->create_collection( ) ##NO_TEXT.

  " map Code property in extended entity to Code property in created value help entity
  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'LocalDataProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_property_path( 'Matnr' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'Material' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'MaterialName' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'MaterialType' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'MaterialTypeName' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'MaterialGroup' ).


  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'MaterialGroupName' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'Plant' ).

  vh_anno_record = vh_collection->create_record( 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly' ).
  vh_anno_property = vh_anno_record->create_property( 'ValueListProperty' ).
  vh_anno_property->create_simple_value( )->set_string( 'PlantName' ).

 Test Your Application: Now you open the application and check the F4 function. 

References: 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3070775
https://me.sap.com/notes/2531364
https://me.sap.com/notes/0002520936
How to redefine RDS based OData services? - SAP Community

entry.title: How to Post Movement Types 313 and 315 Using API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction

Recently I worked on posting stock transfer movements using the standard OData service API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV in SAP S/4HANA. The requirement was to perform transfer posting between storage locations using movement type 313 and 315 through API testing in SAP Gateway Client (/n/IWFND/GW_CLIENT)

During this process, I faced multiple technical and business validation error such as HTT 405, Bad request (400), XML parse issues, and stock in Transfer errors. This blog explains the troubleshooting steps and the final successful payload structure.

Service Used:

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV/A_MaterialDocumentHeader

HTTP Method: POST

Goods Movement Code: 04 (Transfer Posting)

Common Errors Faced and Resolutions:

1. HTT 405 - Method Not Allowed

Cause: POST method executed on wrong URI or service root

Solution: Use the correct entity set:

/A_MaterialDocumentHeader

instead of service root or metadata URL

2. HTTP 400 - System Query Options Error

Cause: Using: ?$format=json

with POST request

Solution: Remove query options from POST URL

3. XML Parse Error

Cause: Incorrect JSON structure for deep insert

Wrong: "to_MaterialDocumentItem": [

Correct: "to_MaterialDocumentItem": {

                  "results": [

Key Learning:

Using standard API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV fro material movement posting is powerful but requirs careful attention to:

  1. Correct entity set
  2. proper JSON structure
  3. Open Posting Periods
  4. Correct business sequence (313 -> 315)

Once these are handled, the API works smoothly and supports seamless automation for stock transfer postings.

Test Case Scenario

Stock Overview before posting 313

(Note: stock available in unrestricted use for material 14000 under batch C1)

Navigate to transaction /n/IWFND/GW_CLIENT and click on service API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV --> Click on SAP Gateway Client

 

Click on Entity Set

Select A_MaterialDocumentHeader and continue

Pass HTTP Request in JSON format to post 313 movement

(Note: make sure you selected POST method, and correct URL)

Movement type 313 is used to move stock into transfer stock.

Sample JSON Payload

{

  "PostingDate": "2026-01-15T00:00:00",

  "GoodsMovementCode": "04",

  "MaterialDocumentHeaderText": "RM01 to WH01",

  "to_MaterialDocumentItem": {

    "results": [

      {

        "Material": "14000",

        "Plant": "2100",

        "StorageLocation": "WH01",

        "GoodsMovementType": "313",

        "QuantityInEntryUnit": "1",

        "EntryUnit": "PC",

        "IssuingOrReceivingPlant": "2100",

        "IssuingOrReceivingStorageLoc": "RM01",

        "Batch": "C1"

      }

    ]

  }

}

Once Executed, HTTP Response displayed with response code 200 (you can find the material document)

Check Stock, it must update in stock in transfer in destination location

Display 313 material document in MIGO transaction

Pass HTTP Request in JSON format to post 315 movement

(Note: make sure you selected POST method, and correct URL)

Movement type 315 is used to clear stock in transfer and complete the movement.

Sample JSON Payload

{

  "PostingDate": "2026-01-15T00:00:00",

  "GoodsMovementCode": "04",

  "MaterialDocumentHeaderText": "Place in RM01",

  "to_MaterialDocumentItem": {

    "results": [

      {

        "Material": "14000",

        "Plant": "2100",

        "StorageLocation": "WH01",

        "GoodsMovementType": "315",

        "QuantityInEntryUnit": "1",

        "EntryUnit": "PC",

        "IssuingOrReceivingPlant": "2100",

        "IssuingOrReceivingStorageLoc": "WH01",

        "Batch": "C1"

      }

    ]

  }

}

Once Executed, HTTP Response displayed with response code 200 (you can find the material document)

Stock updated in Unrestricted Use in destination location under same batch C1

Display 315 Material Document in MIGO transaction

READ posted 313 or 315 document using GET method

(Note: Pass filter URI as mention in below image and mention material document number, and make sure you selected GET method)

Once Executed, HTTP Response displayed with response code 200 (you can display the material document details)

 

If you found this guide helpful, don't miss any other SAP MM WM blogs covering real-world automation, configurations tips, and best practices for procurement, inventory management, and warehouse management.

Click to view other SAP MM WM Blogs - SAP Community

 

Regards,

Mannem Leelamohan

entry.title: Adding Immutable Blockchain Proofs to SAP With CAP - Cardano and OData V4
entry.content:
ODATANO: Turning Cardano into a CAP Plugin

Hi everyone. I'm Max, a SAP developer for 10 years. In my spare time, I've been experimenting with Blockchain and immutable data proofs a lot lately. In particular, with Cardano, because it's one of the few public blockchains built on peer-reviewed research and formal methods, which matters a lot when you're thinking about anchoring any kind of enterprise data. So I tried to work out a way to bring the two worlds together.

This is a write-up of what came out of it. I hope this is useful. Feedback and pushbacks are very welcome.

Why is blockchain next to SAP at all

SAP systems are trusted but mutable. An admin can edit records, a migration can rewrite values, and an audit trail living in the same database as the data it tracks is only as trustworthy as the permissions around it.

A public blockchain solves that one specific problem: immutable, externally verifiable proof that a piece of data existed at a given moment, outside any one organization's trust boundary.

The use cases aren't exotic at all. Audit anchoring, batch traceability, cross-company verification, and regulatory compliance are just a few. So the hard part isn't the use case. It's the integration. Chain-specific SDKs, raw cryptographic payloads, wallet protocols, and provider APIs have nothing in common with the OData services that SAP Systems are used to consuming.

As I got more familiar with CAP, I thought to myself, what if the blockchain were just another data source behind a CAP service?

The CAP reflex

Anyone who's worked with CAP knows the regular pattern. Model the domain in CDS, write service handlers, let the framework handle OData, routing, metadata, and authentication.

Cardano's domain maps surprisingly cleanly to CDS. Blocks, Transactions, UTxOs, Assets, Stake Pools, Epochs are all typed entities with relationships. A Cardano transaction, for example, always has an input and output composition. And one Output has a composition of different Assets. An Asset has policyId, assetName, fingerprint, and quantity. Conceptually, no further from SAP than, say, an external material service with detailed classifications.

If the domain can be modeled, the rest can too.

What ODATANO is

ODATANO is a CAP application that exposes Cardano as three OData V4 services. More importantly, it's packaged as a CAP plugin (@odatano/core on npm). If you've used @cap-js/sqlite or @SAP/cds-hana,  You know the drill: install the package, add a config block, plugin self-registers at startup.

No custom middleware. No manual route wiring. No code changes in your host app.

Setup

npm install @odatano/core @cap-js/sqlite

An example of the “cds” section in a package.jsonconsumer project might look like this:

 
"cds": {
    "requires": {
      "[development]": {
        "auth": { "kind": "dummy"}
      },
      "db": {
        "kind": "sqlite",
        "credentials": { "url": "db.sqlite" }
      },
      "odatano-core": {
        "network": "preview",
        "backends": [ "koios" ],
        "txBuilders": [ "buildooor" ]
      }
    }
  }

The plugin auto-registers and is available after

cds deploy --to sqlite && cds watch

Three OData services are now live alongside your own, same CAP runtime, same auth middleware, full $metadata discovery.

The three services

Read (/odata/v4/cardano-odata) 18 entities: Blocks, Transactions, Addresses, UTxOs, Assets, Stake Pools, Epochs, Accounts, DReps, Protocol Parameters. Full OData V4 query surface: $filter, $select, $expand, $orderby, $top, $skip, $count. 

Transaction (/odata/v4/cardano-transaction) 11 actions covering the full Build/Sign/Submit lifecycle. ADA transfers, multi-asset, native token minting, CIP-20 metadata, Plutus V3. Each action returns a TransactionBuilds entity with fee estimate, unsigned CBOR, and outputs.

Signing (/odata/v4/cardano-sign) 8 actions implementing external signing as a state machine (pending → verified → submitted). CIP-30 wallets, Cardano CLI, PKCS#11 HSMs. The service never touches private keys.

Total: 31 entities, 34 actions, three services.

Why OData & typed entities matter

Every Cardano entity is a proper CDS model with associations and compositions. That changes what consumers can do:

GET /odata/v4/cardano-odata/Transactions('abc123')?$expand=inputs,outputs($expand=assets)

Response is structured and typed. Fiori, Excel, Power BI, custom consumers, nobody has to parse raw blockchain data.

Same principle for actions. Every parameter has a name, type, and annotations. API Business Hub and Gateway Client render input forms from the $metadata document alone.

CAP Architecture patterns

I tried not to invent much. Where CAP had a pattern, I used it.

Service handlers wrap business logic in handleRequest(), which catches blockchain-specific errors and maps them to OData responses. For example, 400 for validation, 404 for missing UTxOs, 503 for backend outages, so service consumers never see a raw chain exception.

Lazy on-demand indexing. No bulk replication. First query for a block, transaction, or address fetches from the backend, persists viaUPSERT in the Database, and serves subsequent requests from cache until TTL expires.

Multi-backend failover. Three backends (Blockfrost, Koios, Ogmios). The orchestrator picks the right one per request type (Ogmios for live UTxOs, Blockfrost for historical) and fails over on timeout. Transparent to the consumer.

Database-agnostic. CDS entities and standard CAP persistence. SQLite locally, HANA on BTP. No code change, no schema migration. CDS compiler handles the DDL.

TRACE: a concrete use case

To make this less abstract and to test the plugin firsthand, I built a second CAP app: TRACE, a Fiori frontend for pharmaceutical supply chain tracking. TRACE just consumes @odatano/core as a plugin and can use all of the available services for interacting with the Cardano blockchain without having to write one line of custom blockchain code.

Business Scenario: A manufacturer produces a batch. Regulation requires tamper-proof evidence of every custody transfer from the manufacturer to the distributor to the pharmacy. Nobody can rewrite history afterward.

The manufacturer mints the batch as an NFT via ODATANOs BuildMintTransaction OData Action. The NFT carries an inline datum (manufacturer ID, batch ID, custody state) written on-chain at creation.

Each Batch handover calls the OData Action BuildPlutusSpendTransaction. The Plutus V3 Cardano Smartcontract validator enforces on-chain who's allowed to transfer. inlineDatumJson carries the updated custody state to the continuing output.

Any party, manufacturer, regulator, pharmacy, or end consumer can read the full custody chain without access to anyone's SAP system. Because the data lives on Cardano, not in any vendor database.

Conclusion

ODATANO started as a personal question: could a public Blockchain like Cardano, with smart contracts & transaction logic, feel like another OData Service? After half a year of building, I think the answer is yes. Three OData services, familiar CAP patterns, and TRACE as proof that a real consumer app doesn't need a single line of blockchain-specific code.  What excites me most is that this way, Cardano just shows up as another service in the stack, with the same $metadata, the same query syntax, the same handlers as any other CAP service, just with tamper-proof storage and cryptographic guarantees by design.

Status

Funded by Cardano Catalyst Fund 14 (Open Developers). Apache 2.0. Final milestone approved late April, no reviewer refusals, about two months before the deadline.

  • Version: v1.7.8 (@odatano/core on npm)
  • Tests: 1,285 tests across 31 suites, 97% statement coverage
  • Cardano Networks: mainnet, preview, preprod
  • Backends: Blockfrost, Koios, Ogmios (with a real Cardano node)
  • CAP compatibility: @SAP/cds 9.x, Node.js 18+
  • Main Repo: github.com/ODATANO/ODATANO
  • TRACE (Example Consumer App):  TRACE 
  • Homepage: https://odatano.dev/ 

If anyone wants to poke at it, try it, tear it apart, please do. I'm especially interested in feedback from people deep in CAP, because I'm never quite sure which patterns are the most idiomatic. Issues, threads, and messages are all welcome.

Thanks and best regards

Max

entry.title: Disabling CSRF Token Validation in SAP for Testing with Postman and External Applications
entry.content:
Introduction

When working with SAP OData services, developers frequently encounter CSRF token validation errors while testing APIs using tools like Postman or integrating with third-party applications. These errors commonly occur during POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE operations because SAP Gateway enforces CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) protection for security purposes.

In this blog, we will walk through:

  • What CSRF token validation is in SAP
  • Why Postman and external applications face CSRF-related issues
  • How to disable CSRF token validation for a specific SAP OData service
  • Testing the service using SAP Gateway Client and Postman

What is CSRF Token Validation in SAP?

CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) protection is a security mechanism implemented by SAP Gateway to prevent unauthorized requests from malicious applications.

For modifying operations such as:

  • POST
  • PUT
  • PATCH
  • DELETE

SAP requires a valid X-CSRF-Token header.

If the token is missing or invalid, SAP returns errors such as:

  • 403 Forbidden
  • CSRF token validation failed

This issue is very common while testing APIs from:

  • Postman
  • External middleware applications
  • Custom UI applications
  • Third-party integrations

Common Issue Faced in Postman

Most developers directly trigger a POST or PUT request from Postman without first fetching the CSRF token.

As a result, SAP Gateway rejects the request due to missing token validation.

Go to transaction code: SICF

SICF ->service->GUI Configuration

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Setting the value to 0 disables CSRF token validation for the service

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Test the Service in SAP Gateway Client:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Testing from Postman

After disabling CSRF validation, you can directly perform:

  • POST requests
  • PUT requests
  • PATCH requests
  • DELETE requests

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

Efficient API testing is essential for successful SAP integrations, and managing CSRF token validation is a key part of that process. Whether you are working with Postman, middleware platforms, or custom applications, knowing how to configure and troubleshoot CSRF settings can save considerable development time. Always ensure that any security relaxation performed for testing is carefully controlled and documented to avoid risks in productive environments.

ABAP Development 

OData 

SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP 

NW ABAP Gateway (OData)

entry.title: Querying SAP SuccessFactors in Natural Language with Claude Desktop and the SF-MCP Server
entry.content:
How to connect an open-source Model Context Protocol server to Claude Desktop and interact with your SuccessFactors tenant using plain English - no OData knowledge required.

What is SF-MCP?

SF-MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that bridges Claude — Anthropic's AI assistant — to SAP SuccessFactors via its OData APIs. Instead of hand-crafting $filter expressions or memorising entity names, you just ask Claude in plain English and it translates your question into the right API call automatically.

 

Example interaction

You: "Who on the Engineering team has a work anniversary this month?"

Claude: Found 3 upcoming anniversaries — Jane Smith (5 years, March 12), 
Bob Johnson (2 years, March 18), Alice Chen (10 years, March 25).

The project lives at github.com/aiadiguru2025/sf-mcp and ships 43 purpose-built HR tools across 13 categories — covering everything from employee lookup and RBP security audits to hiring pipelines, time-off balances, and Integration Center job monitoring.

MCP is an open standard that lets AI assistants connect to external data sources in a secure, standardised way. Claude Desktop is the desktop client that makes it easy to wire up MCP servers locally without any coding.

Prerequisites

Before starting, make sure you have the following:

✅ Claude Desktop installed (download from claude.ai/download)
✅ Python 3.10 or newer — check with python3 --version
✅ Git installed
✅ A SAP SuccessFactors account with API access enabled
✅ Your company ID, data center code (e.g. DC55 for Frankfurt), and API user credentials

Note: Credentials are passed per-request and never stored in the config file. SF-MCP uses per-request authentication, so you will supply your user ID and password when you ask Claude each question.

Step-by-step setup

 

1. Install uv and clone the repository

SF-MCP uses uv, a fast Python package manager. Install it first, then clone the repo.

# Install uv (macOS / Linux)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Install uv (Windows PowerShell)
powershell -c "irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex"

# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/aiadiguru2025/sf-mcp.git
cd sf-mcp

# Install Python dependencies
uv sync

 
2. Find the path to the uv executable

Claude Desktop needs the absolute path to uv to launch the server. Run:

# macOS / Linux
which uv
# → /Users/yourname/.local/bin/uv

# Windows PowerShell
Get-Command uv | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Source
# → C:\Users\yourname\.local\bin\uv.exe

Copy this path — you will need it in the next step.

 
 
3. Edit the Claude Desktop configuration file

Open the config file for your operating system in any text editor:

# macOS
~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

# Windows
%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json

Add the sf-mcp entry inside mcpServers. Replace the paths with the values you found in step 2:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sf-mcp": {
      "command": "/Users/yourname/.local/bin/uv",
      "args": [
        "--directory",
        "/Users/yourname/projects/sf-mcp",
        "run",
        "main.py"
      ]
    }
  }
}
Tip (Windows): Use forward slashes or double backslashes in the JSON paths, e.g. C:\\Users\\yourname\\projects\\sf-mcp
 
4. Restart Claude Desktop and verify the connection

Fully quit Claude Desktop (not just close the window), then reopen it. After a few seconds you should see a hammer icon (🔨) appear in the message input area. Click it — you should see all 43 SF-MCP tools listed.

 
 
5. Run your first query

Now ask Claude a question in plain English. You will need to provide your SuccessFactors connection details the first time — Claude will prompt you for them or you can include them upfront:

 
 

The 43 tools at a glance

SF-MCP groups its tools into 13 categories. Here are some of the most useful ones for day-to-day HR and admin work:

Natural language query examples

Security considerations

Key security features you should be aware of:

🔐 Per-request credentials — your username and password are never stored in the config file or cached to disk. They are passed with each API call and masked in audit logs.
🛡️  Input validation — 10 regex-based validators protect against OData injection. Filter expressions, entity names, and $select/$orderby values are all sanitised.
🧩  XXE-safe XML parsing — the server uses defusedxml to prevent XML external entity attacks when processing OData metadata responses.
📋 Structured audit log — every tool call is logged with a request ID, timestamp, duration, and parameter summary (passwords redacted).

Troubleshooting tips

HTTP 401 — Authentication Error

Verify you are passing the user ID without the @instance suffix. For example use api_admin, not api_admin@ACME_CORP.

Server disconnects immediately in Claude Desktop

Server Disconnects in Claude Desktop

Run which uv again and confirm the path in your config is exactly right. Then test the server manually: uv run mcp dev main.py — this opens the MCP Inspector in your browser so you can call tools interactively.

Rate limit / 429 errors

Rate Limit / 429 Errors

The server automatically retries up to 3 times with a 5-second back-off. If you hit limits frequently, set SF_CACHE_TTL_DEFAULT in a .env file to cache read-heavy responses like picklists and metadata.

Final thoughts

I have created SF-MCP as one of the most complete open-source SuccessFactors API integrations available. Forty-three well-designed tools, solid input validation, production-grade caching and rate limiting, and a clean architecture that is easy to extend, all built on FastMCP, the leading Python MCP framework.

If you are an SF admin, HR tech consultant, or developer who regularly digs through OData APIs, this is worth setting up. The ability to ask Claude "compare User entity config between dev and prod" and get a structured diff instantly is a genuine time-saver.

Give it a ⭐ on GitHub and feel free to contribute - the project is MIT-licensed and I have laid out clear contribution guidelines in the README: github.com/aiadiguru2025/sf-mcp

Disclaimer: 

This project is provided for educational and experimental purposes only. It is offered as-is, with no warranties — express or implied. The author assumes no liability for any damages or issues arising from its use, including data loss, unintended API activity, or security vulnerabilities. Always validate behaviour in a non-production environment before wider use.

Before connecting SF-MCP repo to any SuccessFactors environment, please keep the following in mind:

  • Always start with a sandbox or preview instance - never point this at production as your first test
  • Grant your API user the minimum permissions needed and nothing more
  • Treat all tool outputs as informational — verify before taking any action based on them
  • Every query makes real, live OData API calls to your tenant. There is no simulation or dry-run mode

The author accepts no liability for any damages - direct or indirect - resulting from the use of this software. SF-MCP is an independent community project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or supported by SAP SE or Anthropic.
